copy.keyTakeaway: Tigers edge Rays in a one-run thriller while the Brewers demolish the Giants. Tech stocks mixed as NVDA surges 6.3% and Tesla slides. NBA Finals tip off tomorrow with the Knicks heading to San Antonio.

todaysHits.sports: Tigers walk off Rays; Brewers destroy Giants in rout.
todaysHits.golf: Russell Henley finishes Charles Schwab Challenge at -13.
todaysHits.f1: Monaco Grand Prix this weekend—street circuit chaos incoming.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup 2026 kicks off June 11. 32 teams ready.
todaysHits.culture: Euphoria finale sparks debate. Sweeney, Levinson defend controversial ending.

sportsOther: Tigers scraped one out; Ray pitching imploded in the late innings.
Brewers demolished the Giants—not even close, just a beatdown.
Rockies stole it late; Angels couldn't hold the lead.

markets.mood: Nvidia's having a day — up 6.3% while Tesla got hammered down 4.6% — but the broader market's staying chill with SPY barely moving, which means investors are picking winners instead of betting on everything.
markets.whyBullet1: If your 401k is heavy on mega-cap tech, you just watched Apple drop 1.8% while Microsoft jumped 2.3%, so your diversification either saved you or cost you depending on what you actually own.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch what happens with semiconductor earnings through the end of this week — Nvidia's move today is priced-in optimism, and if guidance disappoints, that 6.3% rally disappears fast.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin's down 4.1% but Nvidia's crushing it anyway, which is wild because people usually treat crypto and big tech rallies as the same trade — apparently the AI infrastructure bet is decoupled from crypto's chaos right now.

f1.headline: Monaco GP This Weekend—Street Circuit Chaos Incoming
f1.whyCare1: Monaco rewards precision over raw speed, and one mistake ends your race in the barriers.
f1.whyCare2: Qualifying here matters more than anywhere else on the calendar because overtaking is nearly impossible.
f1.watchFor: First practice session incidents—they'll reveal which teams nailed setup and which ones are already scrambling.
f1.whatToSay: Monaco separates the drivers who can handle pressure from the ones who crack when there's no margin for error.

culture.topic: Sydney Sweeney shuts down Euphoria haters
culture.whatHappened: Sydney Sweeney responded to critics of Euphoria Season 3 with a three-word response that cut through all the noise.
culture.whyItMatters: The show's finale sparked real debate, and watching an actor stand her ground instead of apologizing says something about where TV discourse is heading.
culture.whatToSay: She just kept it short and didn't engage with every take—that's the move.

culture.topic: Mackenzie Phillips lived through the cocaine era
culture.whatHappened: Mackenzie Phillips opened up about wild cocaine use during her sitcom days and working alongside another co-star in that world.
culture.whyItMatters: It's a rare look at how normalized drug use was on sets in that era, and how differently we treat addiction now versus then.
culture.whatToSay: That whole generation of TV was operating under totally different rules.

culture.whatHappened: Ryan Coogler's supernatural horror film set in 1930s Mississippi Delta.
